entry.title: Re: Issue while accessing the Ariba application in BTP wo...
entry.content: Hi @HakanHaslaman, Thanks for the providing me with the troubleshooting steps and I've have requested Basis team for the support, will check with the team and update incase the issue persists.

entry.title: Re: Rearrange fields on the Workflow Approval Page
entry.content:
Hi Esteban,

We are facing similar issue with custom MDF. The fields are vanished and the order has changed in the Manage workflow request page. Kindly help us as to how we can rearrange the order for Custom MDF. The UI and the other object related are all the same with respect to both Production and Preview. Only the fields in the Manage Workflow request page has got de-aligned. Please help!!

 

Regards,

Nagavalli B N

entry.title: Re: Sending Customer invoice to Multiple recipients in S/...
entry.content: Hi Vivek, Thank you for providing very useful documentation. My Client looking for Standard functionality in BRF+ to send customer invoice to Multiple Email id's maintained in Business Partner.

entry.title: BYD2411 How to set Traditional Chinese as system language
entry.content:
Currently, the BYD tenant is only available for Chinese and English. We need Traditional Chinese.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2411 How to set Traditional Chinese as system lang...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Please following the steps in this KBA to do the configuration.

https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/1777108

 

Best Regards,

Cariford

entry.title: How to Utilize an OData Service in Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are trying to utilize an OData service that is used for an application that is part of the SAP public cloud Launchpad, we have identified the Service name from the Fiori Apps library and we would like to utilize it to create custom API's/ custom arrangements in Public cloud to use data from Public cloud service in BTP.

Kindly guide on how we can utilize the Odata services on public cloud platform.

Sample app and service info:

Odata service identified: LO_VCHCLF_SIMULATION_SRV

Application Name: Simulate Configuration Models (F2570)

Thank you in advance

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition  OData

entry.title: Re: IPS transformation assignGroup and multiple groups assignment
entry.content:
@ThomasRauen 

Thank you for answer. First check if only for group id assignment. Second check is only to be sure that $.groups isn't empty  to avoid an error. 

OnPrem users have few groups, not all need to be assigned to user in IAS. (eg, group Z_UPDATE_UUID is used only as a filter, for global ID sync between IAS and OnPrem systems for Sap Task Cockpit)

Groups are used in conditional authentication to some apps. 

Groups can't be created, deleted by replication from any source, only update is allowed. Groups can by only added or removed from user. 

Standard group mapping, rewrites groups and all assignments made manually or via replica from other systems are removed. I was trying to use skip 

 

    "group": {
        "skipOperations": [
            "create",
            "delete"
        ],
        "mappings": [
            {
                "sourceVariable": "entityIdTargetSystem",
                "targetPath": "$.id"
            },

 


But then I'm getting a message that groups can't be updated

 

As explained in 2708752 - Entities Aren't Updated in the Target System In Identity Provisioning Service  this isn't a bug but a feature. 

We have followed https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/enabling-group-assignment?locale=en-US&q=groups 

But there is some issue with group deletion 

if this code is used 

 

            {
                "condition": "$.groups[?(@.value == 'Z_TEST_DEV')] EMPTY true",
                "constant": [
                    {
                        "id": "xxxxx-xxxx-xxxxx-9aaf-877d9b25f806"
                    }
                ],
                "targetVariable": "unassignGroup"
            }

 

Error is generated (ticket to sap raised) 

These are the reasons we try to assign groups this way. Maybe I'm wrong, I'm still looking for a better solution:)

Thank you for your hint. 

A.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud SDK: How to setup requests using the busine...
entry.content: Try setting up the destination as described in documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/user-propagation-from-cloud-foundry-environment-to-sap-s-4hana-cloud?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: 公司间销售业务的按单生产业务
entry.content: Hi @Vivek_Savant , Thanks for your reply . If the 6GC support WBS too ?

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Scope: What Happens after 2025/2030
entry.content:
Hi Hiroshi

 

SAP won't charge any penalties as no contractual penalties are agreed in the contract - however using compa pack features after 2025 means a breach of contract - may leading to potential consequences

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Import Bank statement in SAP S4 HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi Yogita, @Yogita_Kadam 

Have you had a look at the following Help document, yet? --> https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/9929e3049c88402a8d56e437a3967e38.html?locale=en-US 

Under the first section "How do bank statements get into your system?", the text explains the different options to import/uplod bank statements into your SAP-S/4HANA-Cloud-based system.

I hope this helps. 

Best regards,

Anita

entry.title: Re: Sending Customer invoice from multiple E-mail Address...
entry.content:
Hello, 

I assume the question is same as the one posted a few days ago - 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sending-customer-invoice-to-multiple-recipients-in-s-4-hana-with-brf/qaq-p/13951478

entry.title: Re: Proration and Number Formats in Variable Pay
entry.content:
Hello @xavierlegarrec,

I performed your steps but without success, the system keeps on rounding Proration (my intention was to have at least 4 decimal places) although I changed the defProrationformat to: "#,##0.00##" and double checked in XML that Proration is connected to the format. I played also with other combinations of 0 and # but it didn't help. 

Could you re-test the configuration from your video and confirm if it's still valid? I start to believe that this might be a system issue after the last release.

Many thanks!

entry.title: Re: Regarding the sap systems and non-sap systems
entry.content:
Hi,
This does not look like a SAP CPQ question. Can you please update product tag to better reflect the topic. Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps: App doesnt get user information
entry.content: I checked the response so in the deployed version the systemVars.currentUser.email changes to systemVars.currentUser.name. Replace it with name and its should be working.

entry.title: 回复： Integration of Solution Orders with Customer Projects: Mapping of Accounting Data
entry.content: Nice blog! It really helps me to understand the behavior!

entry.title: Re: 公司间销售业务的按单生产业务
entry.content:
Hello @omega-ou 

Yes, it also supports WBS. However, please note that this scope item requires an additional license.

Please check and let me know.

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Intra-plant and cross-plant view on MD04
entry.content:
Please take a look at the following article:

Improvements for MRP areas in S/4HANA - Intra-Plan... - SAP Community

This view can be activated in the following customizing path:

SPRO: Production - Material Requirements Planning - Evaluation - Display Material Groupings

entry.title: Re: Set plant internal view as default in the MD04
entry.content: Did you get a solution for setting the intra-plant view as default in MD04?

entry.title: Re: Service Contract/Service Order Text type
entry.content:
Hi Soma,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can't config custom text types for service contract/service order. If you need special text types, you can leave more details here or use SAP Continuous Influence to submit an improvement.

Thanks.

Best regards,

Wei

entry.title: Re: Quality Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud – Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA)
entry.content: Thank you for this helpful Blog Post. Is there any SAP Best Practice Tutorial regarding FMEA?I have not been able to find it in the Processnavigator Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Partitioning based on Change Number Range
entry.content:
Hi, referring to the specific questions:

1. You can not take the CHANGENR specified in the example but need to take the correct one for you system queried from CDHDR based on your desired starting date.
2. MAX_CHANGENR does NOT need to be defined specifically. It will be created by the set pagesize / when no data is available anymore in a page.

On top of that since our last release you can look into automated pagination which makes it obsolete to use Adv. Extraction here.
Please refer to this documentation page:
https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-intelligence/user-guide/pagination?locale=en-US

By using this feature and setting a SQL Filter which looks like this you are able to run an initial extraction via standard extraction with automated pagination:

OBJECTCLAS IN (<specify neccessary objectclas>)
AND CHANGENR >= ( SELECT min(changenr) FROM CDHDR WHERE udate >= '$delta' AND OBJECTCLAS IN (<specify neccessary objectclas>))
Example:

 



Then you would just need to add an dynamic time variable (called "delta) containing your desired start date from where the data should be extracted.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Partitioning based on Change Number Range
Re: What are the options for restoring a project version...
entry.content: Thanks Jana. I understand that you have quarantined the Decision artifacts in a different project. This allows for restoring the rest of the project. I am primarily interested in restoring the project containing Decisions. Is there a way to restore a project with Decisions to a previous state? It not about deploying a previous version, I want to make changes from a previous version as a catastrophic change was done later.

entry.title: Re: Provision users from Microsoft Azure AD to SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication
entry.content:
Hi Experts
Can I have two different Azure AD as sources?

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: I need to make a copy of Systema PI Netweaver 7.5
entry.content:
Hi,

It is a bit confusing when you mention the system is PI 7.5 and EHP8.

Also, I'm not sure what you mean, when you say copying a system using sap GUI.

If you need to create a system that replicates your current PI system version 7.5, please refer to the first note. It will provide you with a comprehensive guide to help you through the entire process. I hope you find this helpful.

2574603 - PI: Official SAP Guide Finder

3051861 - Which CTC Configuration Tasks (Wizards) do I need to execute in my PI/PO systems and when do I execute them?

And please open one thread for one request/issue and close the duplicate.

Regards,

Prithviraj

entry.title: Is it necessary to use mes-driven staging ?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Is it necessary to use mes-driven staging if I want to consume material in DM via EWM goods issue?

In the diagram, MES inventory is created through Warehouse Request and Warehouse task.
By the way, you can just skip staging request and creating warehouse request to download inventory to DM.

So, I was wondering it's possible to consume material in DM without MES-driven staging.

https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/execution/staging-request-manage-staging?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/execution/component-consumption-work-center-pod-52e298580bcc40f988f738e86fe835aa-52?locale=en-US

Best regards,
Koichi Okamoto

entry.title: Learning RAP100 tutorials with permission related issues
entry.content:
According to the tutorial to learn RAP, learn that the steps to use ADT to open Launchpad pages is prompted “You do not have permission to access the Manage Launchpad Pages App”, how can I solve this problem?

tutorial link: step 2

Many thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: BW Series – Getting Started (Part 2)
entry.content: great content.

entry.title: Lead Target Creation
entry.content:
Hi all,

Can we create a "Lead Target" under the "Sales Target Planning" section.

I am aiming to create the same process as sales target planning however I want to make my sales manager able to create "lead target" insted of "sales target" for the sales representatives.

For example I want to create the target as "hit the target of creating 15 leads for the next two months"

How can I manage the process?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Where are my blogs I have linkedin my IDs and now what I have is a new id with no historical dat
entry.content: Please search first in the existing same questions e.g. Solved: How to find all my blog posts in SAP Community 2024 - SAP Community

entry.title: Is FSCM available in SAP Public cloud?
entry.content:
Hi,

Is FSCM available in SAP Public cloud? if yes, what is the scope item.

On checking the "Digital discovery" could not find any. I am not looking for BD6 or 1QM.

Best Regards,

Dave

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps: App doesnt get user information
entry.content:
Hi @anmolbhatia003,

I have solve it by using a delay between the call and using the variable.

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client
entry.content:
Hi @patrick_winkler ,
In S4 HANA Public Cloud 

Therefore, if the parameter "adaptable in production" is set to false, the user will not be able to edit the configuration even if he/she has the required authorization. If set to true, the user can edit the configuration. 

Where exactly this will be available to configure ? is there any specific application ? If so kindly let us know the same. 

Regards,
Shyam

entry.title: Import Credit Memo Requests – Introduction
entry.content:
Start from SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408.1 (HFC5), a new app Import Credit Memo Requests is delivered, you can now easily import credit memo requests from a spreadsheet file and this article will provide some tips about using this app.

1. How to Access

To access this app, business users need to have the following business catalog assigned: Sales - Credit Memo Request Import (SAP_SD_BC_CMR_IMPORT_PC).

2. How to Use

Generic functions such as downloading template, preview check and so on, are the same with other import apps like Import Sales Orders. You can find the detailed usage of the app series by referring to the link.

Supported credit memo request types:

Document Type

Creation Without Reference

Creation With Reference

CR

Yes

Sales order, invoice

RK

No

Invoice

CMR4

Yes

Invoice

3. Creation with Reference

By importing data of preceding documents, you can create credit memo requests with reference to sales orders or invoices. The referenced data can be header data, item data, or both.

The following table lists the fields used for document reference. You can download the import template and add relevant fields to your credit memo request data sheet.

Data Level

Field

Description

Used For

Header

Reference document (HEADER.REFERENCESDDOCUMENT)

Document number of the preceding document

Header reference

Item

Reference document (ITEM.REFERENCESDDOCUMENT)

Document number of the preceding document

Item reference

3.1 Reference Example

3.1.1 Only Header Reference

Example: You create a credit memo request with reference to sales order 10060. The credit memo request header references the sales order, whereas the two items are created without reference.

Credit Memo Request (Temporary ID)

Reference Document (for Header)

Item (Temporary ID)

Reference Document (for Item)

Reference Item

1001

10060

01

  

1001

10060

02

  

Result:

Document Number

Item  

Reference Document

(Preceding Document)

Reference Item

(Item of Preceding Document)

0060001001

 

10060

 

0060001001

10

 

 

0060001001

20

 

 

3.1.2 Same Document Referenced on Header and Item Level

Example: You create a credit memo request with reference to sales order 10060. The credit memo request header references the sales order. The first item references item 10 of the sales order, whereas the second item is created without reference.

Credit Memo Request (Temporary ID)

Reference Document (for Header)

Item (Temporary ID)

Reference Document (for Item)

Reference Item

1002

10060

01

 

10

1002

10060

02

  

Result:

Document Number

Item  

Reference Document

(Preceding Document)

Reference Item

(Item of Preceding Document)

0060001002

 

10060

 

0060001002

10

10060

10

0060001002

20

 

 

 

3.1.3 Different Documents Referenced on Header and Item Level

Example:  You create a credit memo request with reference to sales orders 10060 and 10070. The credit memo request header references sales order 10060. The first item references item 10 of sales order 10070, whereas the second item is created without reference.

 

Credit Memo Request (Temporary ID)

Reference Document (for Header)

Item (Temporary ID)

Reference Document (for Item)

Reference Item

1003

10060

01

10070

10

1003

10060

02

  

Result:

Document Number

Item  

Reference Document

(Preceding Document)

Reference Item

(Item of Preceding Document)

0060001003

 

10060

 

0060001003

10

10070

10

0060001003

20

 

 

 

3.1.4 Only Item Reference

Example: You create a credit memo request with reference to sales order 10060. The credit memo request header is created without reference. The first item references item 10 of sales order 10060, whereas the second item is created without reference.

Credit Memo Request (Temporary ID)

Reference Document (for Header)

Item (Temporary ID)

Reference Document (for Item)

Reference Item

1004

 

01

10060

10

1004

 

02

  

Result:

Document Number

Item  

Reference Document

(Preceding Document)

Reference Item

(Preceding Document Item)

0060001004

 

 

 

0060001004

10

10060

10

0060001004

20

 

 

 

3.2 Data Entry Instructions

Due to document reference, you can leave the fields marked in blue empty and must specify the fields marked in yellow.

3.2.1 Header fields

With header reference, you can leave the sales area (sales organization, distribution channel, division) and sold-to party empty. The system ignores the sales area in your file and copies it from the preceding document. You can specify the sold-to party if needed.

The following table compares how you can enter header data for creation with or without reference.

Reference or Not

Credit Memo Request (Temporary ID)

Credit Memo Request Type

Sales Organization

Distribution Channel

Division

Sold-  to Party

Reference Document (for Header)

Without reference

1001

CR

1010

10

00

5001

 

With header reference

1002

CR

 

 

 

 

200001

3.2.2 Item fields

With item reference, you can leave the product-related fields (product, customer material, and GTIN (EAN/UPC)) and the target quantity empty. The system ignores the product and GTIN in your file and copies these fields from the preceding item. If needed, you can specify the customer material and target quantity.

The following table compares how you can enter item data for creation with or without reference.

Reference or Not

Item (Temporary ID)

Product

Customer Material

GTIN (EAN/UPC)

Target Quantity

Reference Document (for Item)

Reference Item

Without reference

01

TG11

 

 

1

 

 

With item reference (no quantity specified)

02

 

 

 

 

20001

10

With item reference (quantity specified)

03

 

 

 

5

20001

20

3.2.3 Target Quantity

If you do not specify a target quantity in your file, the credit memo request will use invoiced quantity of the preceding item as the target quantity. If the invoiced quantity of the preceding item is empty, the target quantity is 0.

4. Special Cases

4.1 Sold-to Party of Preceding Document Not Referenced (Same Document Referenced on Header and Item Level)

Objective: The same document is referenced on header and item level. You want to use a sold-to party that differs from that of the preceding document.

Rule: For your credit memo request items, enter item numbers of the preceding document in ascending order.

Example: Sales order 10060 is referenced and its sold-to party is 10100006. You want to create a credit memo request with reference to sales order 10060 and the sold-to party of the credit memo request is 10100008.

 

Credit Memo Request (Temporary ID)

Sold to Party

(Header)

Reference Document (for Header)

Item (Temporary ID)

Reference Document (for Item)

Reference Item

1001

10100008

10060

01

 

20

1001

10100008

10060

02

 

30

1001

10100008

10060

03

 

50

 

Hint: If the item numbers are not properly entered, there will be an error message: Deviating sold-to parties 10100008 vs. 10100006 (ref. doc. 10060).

4.2 Sales Area of Preceding Document Not Referenced (Same Document Referenced on Header and Item Level)

Same requirement as sold-to party.

4.3 Bills of Material (BOM)

The following rules apply to creation of credit memo requests of document type CR with reference to sales orders.

4.3.1 Only Main Item of the BOM Referenced

Objective: You want to create credit memo request items with reference to BOM main items in different sales orders.

Example: Item 10 in sales order 10060 is the main item of a BOM, and item 20 in sales order 10070 is the main item of another BOM. These items are referenced to create credit memo request items.

Credit Memo Request (Temporary ID)

Item (Temporary ID)

Reference Document (for Item)

Reference Item

Preceding Item material

1001

01

10060

10

Root BOM 1

1001

02

10070

10

Root BOM 2

4.3.2 Main Item and Subitems of the BOM Referenced

Objective: You want to create credit memo request items with reference to the main item and subitems of a BOM.

Rule: For your credit memo request items (temporary IDs in ascending order), enter the item number of the BOM main item first, followed by item numbers (in ascending order) of the BOM subitems.

Example: In sales order 10060, item 10 is the main item of a BOM, items 20 to 40 are BOM subitems. These items (except item 20) are referenced to create credit memo request items.

Credit Memo Request (Temporary ID)

Item (Temporary ID)

Reference Document (for Item)

Reference Item

Reference Item material

1001

01

10060

10

Root BOM

1001

02

10060

30

Component BOM 2

1001

03

10060

40

Component BOM 3

 

4.4 Target Quantity for Document Type RK (Invoice Correction Request)

When you create an invoice correction request with reference to an invoice, two invoice correction request items will be created for each item in your file. The quantity of the first item will be the same as that of the invoiced item (that is, the referenced item), and the quantity of the second item takes the target quantity of the item in your file.

Example:

Credit Memo Request (Temporary ID)

Item (Temporary ID)

Reference Document (for Item)

Reference Item

Target Quantity

1001

01

90060

10

  1

Result:

Credit Memo Request

Item

Reference Document

Reference Item

Target Quantity

0060001001

000010

90060

10

  100

0060001001

000020

90060

10

  1

5. Useful links

Import Credit Memo Requests

App Extensibility: Import Credit Memo Requests

Blog Series for Import Sales Documents

entry.title: Re: How to add exception handling in SAP HANA CURSOR.
entry.content:
Is this question related to the SAP SQL Anywhere database system? If not, I'd suggest to remove the "SAP SQL Anywhere" tag.

I am not familiar with SAP HANA SQL but you might just specify that your self-referencing inner join excludes rows where both IDs are identical, say (assuming the "MyTable" table has rows for parent as ParentID and child simply as ID):

...MyTable P inner join MyTable C on P.ID = C.ParentID and P.ID != C.ID...

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter December 2024
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here an overview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Preview available live sessions on SAP Support Portal, tailored per region:
APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America*

Activate Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Joule revolutionizes how you interact with your SAP business systems, making every touchpoint count and every task simpler. Would you like to start using on a daily basis? If so, we are piloting a new service to help you with that.
Learn more

Customer Insights Dashboard now Part of SAP for Me

We are excited to announce that the planned transition from the SAP Enterprise Support Reporting Cockpit to the new SAP for Me reporting has been completed and now the new interactive analytical Customer Insights dashboard in SAP for Me is fully replacing the SAP ES reporting cockpit.
All about the move

In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

We’re happy to announce that the product deep-dive enablement series is ready to roll out again. The series aims to provide SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition product deep-dive knowledge, independently from the release update.
Mark your calendar for December 9 to get the latest update.
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Series

E-Learning: SAP Analytics Cloud BI Story Builder

Check out this new E-Learning focused purely on the basics of developing an efficient and effective BI Story in SAP Analytics Cloud. Included in the Reference Materials section of the E-Learning you can find hands-on materials such as sample data file, sample scripts, tutorial exercises and more.
Details in this blog

New! SAP Design to Operate: Strategy of Each Pillar E-learning

SAP offers solutions and strategy to digitalize and enhance the efficiency of supply chain operations. For insights, we‘re pleased to share SAP Design to Operate: Strategy of Each Pillar e-learning. This E-learning provides an overview of the SAP Design to Operate lines of business, covering Design, Plan, Manufacture, Deliver, and Operate, and offers a comprehensive understanding of end-to-end supply chain operations.
Access the elearning (value maps access required)

SAP S/4HANA Finance Fundamentals – Deep Dive

Join us in a multiday training session to discover everything you need to know about delta capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Finance. We offer two sessions in December, on Dec 2 and 9.
More in this blog post

How to Get Started With SAP S/4HANA Finance

Are you about to start with SAP S/4HANA Finance? Then don’t miss our interactive virtual Live Must-Know session that lasts less than 2 hours on December 9 that helps you get on board.
Register through this blog post: Get started with SAP S/4HANA Finance

SAP’s Built-In Support: When Real-Time Support Meets Compliance for Life Sciences Supply Chain

Built-In Support is changing the way assistance is provided for users in the life sciences supply chain. Want to know how? Don’t miss this interview featuring Tarun Luthra and Andreas Krummlauf about traceability of life-saving medicines and how streamlined tracking processes enhance user experience and support efficiency.
sap.to/61108SzXf4

The Evolution of Customer Support in Software: Harnessing AI and LLMs to Drive Excellence

Explore the future of customer support! Learn how AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) are transforming traditional support, driving proactive solutions, and personalizing experiences. Companies like SAP are leading the way to predict issues, boost response times, and create stronger customer relationships. Check it out to see how AI-powered insights are reshaping Customer Support.
Read the full article in LinkedIn

Introducing Business Impact-Driven Case Prioritization

We wanted to let you know about a recent change we've made in the ‘Get Support’ application from SAP for Me. The key change is the shift from prioritizing issues based on levels to describing the business impact of the issue. This will directly determine the priority of your case, leading to more accurate and responsive support.
sap.to/61100SmsFY

Companies That Get Customer Orientation Right

Customer orientation is vital for retention, especially when things don’t go as planned. In his latest article, Stefan Steinle, Executive Vice President, Head of Customer Support & Cloud Lifecycle Management at SAP, discusses how brands like Singapore Airlines, Amazon, and Swiggy deliver customer-centricity at every step. Their thoughtful service, even during challenges, helps turn customers into advocates. At SAP, a similar approach is taken, where exceptional support helps clients like Audi set new standards for excellence.
Click here for the article

Replay the virtual SAP Enterprise Support Day

Couldn’t make it out to the event? Experience it again! Explore SAP Enterprise Support Day 2024 sessions, presentations and FAQs in a variety of different languages, anytime.
Check out the event recap

  • Guidance on signing up
  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy Video
  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy at a glance
  • Direct access to SAP Learning Hub (Login with your s-user ID and password)
  • SAP ES Academy Entitlement document
  • FAQ
  • SAP Enterprise Support value maps
  • SAP Support Portal
  • Basics: Sign up | Time zone setup |
  • Formats: EGI | MTE | Program
  • Search: Language | Keyword | Filter | Calendar
  • Upcoming live sessions in your region: APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America

entry.title: Integrate PIR´s from IBP to S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to integrate the Final Demand Planning Qty from IBP to a Public Cloud System by using the best practice integration flow. Integration itself is working, however the PIRs coming from IBP are not integrated as the active version, meaning I have to activate the PIR version by hand in the Public Cloud System. Is this the intended procedure or is there a way to integrate the PIRs as the active version?

Also, after deploying the integration flow and then changing the Final Demand Planning Qty in IBP and deploying the flow again, the PIRs that have previously been integrated are not updated with the new numbers. Is there a setting to enable the PIRs to be overwritten?

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: What is an app for budget and commitment carry forwar...
entry.content:
Hello,

the budget availability control for WBS elements uses either a yearly or a project lifetime budget. It is not foreseen to carry forward budget from one fiscal year to the next one. Therefore, no carry forward app for these amounts is available. 

Best regards,

Marei

entry.title: CF deploy mtar file without deleting already deployed applications
entry.content:
We have a project with many fiori elements apps which are deployed on BTP using mtar, but if we build an mtar with only some of the apps, after deployment the missing apps from the mtar are removed from BTP.

How can we deploy some apps without others to be deleted? Is there something like 
cf deploy mta-ui-apps.mtar --do-not-delete  or similar flag?

entry.title: Re: Where are my blogs I have linkedin my IDs and now wha...
entry.content:
Hi @vinitakasliwal - welcome back to the community 🙂

We migrated to a new platform earlier this year.
If your community profile had not been linked to your Universal ID prior to the migration, your profile was anonymised as part of the migration for privacy reasons. 

Please send us an email at community@sap.com, we can help you recover your old profile and content. 

/Anne

entry.title: Re: Automatic HU WT create within storage control
entry.content:
BTW: the sequence you show for your POSC (the steps ZQIS / IB02 / IB03) is wrong (assuming IB02 and IB03 are the "standard" steps deconsolidation and putaway).

The sequence does not work this way, this is also documented in the node documentation:

"You can only enter internal storage process steps in the following sequence.
For the putaway process:
a) Unload
b) Count
c) Deconsolidate
d) Quality Inspection
e) Perform Value-Added Services
f) Put Away"

The system does not check the entries you make, and you might come away with some different sequences - but I also would not think that a deconsolidation step makes sense after a quality inspection (especially because a deconsolidation comes before quality inspection when you need a sample for QM only).

Brgds

Juergen

entry.title: Re: Working with the Attachment plugin - Cloud Application Programming
entry.content: @arunkrishnamoorthy1988 can you also share how we can utilize this plugin using UI5 freestyle applications? or is it only limited to applications built using annotation?

entry.title: Re: BAS : "code: not found"
entry.content:
Hi @yannmiquel,

 

What worked for me was to disable VS Code as terminal git editor. Git then just warns me about merge conflicts and asks to resolve them manually, which is fine for me because I'll do that in VS Code / BAS Source Control view anyway.

Warning: I was not able to make VS Code / BAS open any editor though, even after re-enabling that setting.

In BAS:

  • open settings (e.g. via "Preferences: Open Settings (UI)")
  • search for setting "Terminal Git Editor" (setting id "git.terminalGitEditor")
  • disable that setting
  • restart / open new terminal

On merge conflicts git now only shows this message:

 

Auto-merging test.txt
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in test.txt
Automatic merge failed; fix conflicts and then commit the result.

 

Kind regards,
Tobias

entry.title: Re: SAP TPM — Trading Partner configuration
entry.content:
Hi Jyoti,

Thank you.
Although ECC is not listed among Type Systems, I don't see any reason why not. TPM cares about the IDoc, not about the system which generated it. As long as SAP ECC can call the IDoc Sender adapter in Integration suite, which it can, it should work.
With regards,
Jiri

entry.title: Crystal Reports: nvarchar(max) fields truncating after 255 characters in runtime
entry.content:
Hi there,
our database previously used the Native Client 11 as a driver and our IT Support said they would no longer support it and requested for the manufacturer of the database to allow for the ODBC 18 Driver. They did this but then the memo fields in all our Crystal Reports were getting truncated using Crystal Reports 2016. The Database Manufacturer suggested update to Crystal Reports 2020. We installed a trial version and when I, as database admin, export the reports using the Crystal program I have no issue. However most of our database users work directly in the client and export the reports using runtime and the problem still exists - that nvarchar(MAX) fields are getting truncated.

Is this a bug in the runtime version? The database manufacturer said it is a problem only SAP can resolve.

entry.title: Re: Deployment error in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi, 

If that is the case, do check the assigned roles and role collections to your user.

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/authorizations

If this doesn't work. You can raise a ticket for more accurate response. 

I hope this helps.

 

Thanks 

Weisson

entry.title: Implementing Address Typeahead using SAP Data Quality Services for SuccessFactors Address portlet
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

We are in process of implementing Address Typeahead solution utilizing SAP BTP service SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data in SuccessFactors Employee Central Address Information. We are doing some impact assessment before starting the implementation, to check whether this solution will fit for Ericsson landscape or not. We have few queries, need to your help to clarify the same.

 

Q1. For Address Typeahead, The JSON file (SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central - Standard Typeahead Configuration - BEL BRA DEU NLD USA file) with the preconfigured local version mappings was offered by SAP to kick start the implementation. But this file only provide configuration standard data model delivered by SAP for the home address in the country/region specific Succession Data Model (CSF SDM). For our customer, standard configurations were not followed, we have custom configurations in Home address. Is it possible to do this kind of custom configurations in SAP DQM Address Typeahead services by following custom configurations ? I have attached the screenshot of configurations of USA country for your reference.

 

Q2. We have more than 100+ countries configured in our customer SF environment, do we need to do the individual country wise separate mapping in SAP DQM Address Typeahead services configurations? or if few countries follows same configurations then can we utilize the 'default' mapping, instead of country specific separate mapping in SAP DQM services?

 


Q3. We would like to utilize the Address Typeahead solution for all countries, so if we enable the service in SuccessFactors provisioning "Employee Central Address Validation Service" under company setting then this will be enough ? or do we need to configure the 'Address Validation Configuration For Country/Region' under manage data for all countries separately?

 

Q4. How to transport the mapping configurations from one to another environment in SAP DQM? Or do we need to manually configure everything in higher environment again?

entry.title: Re: CER006 exam attempts payment instructions
entry.content: @Vijaychembun Could you please share which steps you took to complete the process?

entry.title: Re: CER006
entry.content:
Hi @OmarKhalid1 - the certification exams have moved to learning.sap.com, you can still purchase the exam attempts there. (both for CER001 and CER006)

You will find the direct links in the disclaimer on top of training.sap.com.

entry.title: Re: Query with SAP Note 3508244 - Incompatible Change in...
entry.content: Thanks Robert, we will raise the SAP OSS incident for this. Just wanted to check if anyone faced similar issue

entry.title: Re: Trouble getting extracted line items to show in an approval form
entry.content:
Hello,

The response was in object json and list within a list:

Should be:

"yourResponseVariable[0].amount[0].value[0].toString()"

also, you need to perform a For Each, or custom script to re-assign the values properly to the DataType you created.

entry.title: Is it possible to create multiple entries with the same key in the source list?
entry.content:
Hi!

We have an issue with our SAP public cloud system that we will need to create multiple entries with the same key in the source list.

We are aware that we get error "System message 06 329" when we try to crate multiple entries with the same key. In private cloud or On-premise, we were able to change the "error" into "warning" or "information" but we are not sure if that is possible in the public cloud system as well.

Could anyone tell us if it is possible to convert this error into "warning" or "information" and enable creation of entries with the same key on the source list?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client
entry.content:
@ShyamPrasad 
"Adaptable in Production" is an attribute of the transport object. You can edit them using ABAP Development Tools, see also my last comment.

I recommend using the ADT wizard to create such an app with the "No Transport" option.

entry.title: Re: BAPI for Material storage location wise stock check
entry.content: MARD will give storage location stock, but not stock available at storage location according to ATP logic.

entry.title: Re: BAPI for Material storage location wise stock check
entry.content: Please, don't confuse ATP (BAPI_MATERIAL_AVAILABILITY) with stock/requirements list (BAPI_MATERIAL_STOCK_REQ_LIST) or storage location stock (MARD).

entry.title: Re: BAPI for Material storage location wise stock check
entry.content: What do you really need? Stock availability (ATP) at storage location or storage location stock?

entry.title: Re: Shared Folder Path in Automatic Creation of Sales Orders from Excel (4G5)
entry.content:
Yes you can use a shared filepath as long as it is not a sharepoint path or cloud filepath. 

For the requirement, I assume it will be dependent on the data that you'll be saving. On from the infra perspective. here is the link: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/initial-setup

entry.title: Re: Where are my blogs I have linkedin my IDs and now wha...
entry.content: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/rows-and-column-limit-on-fiori-apps/ba-p/13524715

entry.title: ADD CUSTOM FIELDS TO ACCOUNT ASSIGNMENT IN MIGO
entry.content:
Hello SAP HELPERS , 
I have a request from my client and its urgent so please anyone to help me with the exact steps . 

i don't want to add a tab i want to modify the existing TAB (area)  ACCOUNT ASSIGNMENT . 
below you can find what i mean 

I have already appended the field in GOITEM structure and the MSEG structure with two fields :
ZDATEDE TYPE BUDAT 
AND 
ZDATE A TYPE BUDAT 
i tried to implement the BADI migo in the PBO_DETAIL method but i can't find the way to add these fields to this exact position..
I want also t know how to handle to to post the value of these fields in the MSEG

entry.title: Missing mode selector and infinite loading when accessing a Composable Storefront in SmartEdit
entry.content:
When accessing a Composable Storefront page in SmartEdit, the mode selector (Preview, Basic Edit and Advanced Edit) is missing along with an infinite loading screen. In some cases, below errors can be observed in the browser console tab:

  • ERROR Error: Error: the page is not a valid storefront page.
  • Uncaught Error: https:/smarteditDomain is not allowed to override this storefront

This situation is caused by a misconfiguration in allowOrigins in smart-edit-feature.module.ts file. Set the SmartEdit Web Site domain in allowOrigin in the smart-edit-feature.module.ts file, as per the below example:

provideConfig(<SmartEditConfig>{
   smartEdit: {
     storefrontPreviewRoute: 'your-preview-route-value',
     allowOrigin: '<SmartEdit Web Site domain>',
   },
 })

entry.title: Re: Where are my blogs I have linkedin my IDs and now wha...
entry.content: @AnnePetteroe thanks for your response that is helpful I have emailed and adding details here as well This is my old ID p1404824327 with email vinita17.03@gmail.com and my new is with this email Vinita.kasliwal@peritossolutions.com

entry.title: Re: Where are my blogs I have linkedin my IDs and now wha...
entry.content: Also note all my previous contribution show as Former member hope they can be attributed to me

entry.title: Comparative Analysis of Data Selection Techniques in SAP
entry.content:
In SAP systems, selecting the right data extraction method plays a pivotal role in ensuring system efficiency and performance. With vast amounts of data stored in SAP databases, it's essential to choose the right method for data retrieval, especially to keep operations responsive and well-optimized.

In this article, we will explore various SAP data selection techniques such as the Open Cursor, Core Data Services (CDS) views, Database views, and some other advanced strategies. Each has its strengths and weaknesses and understanding them will help you determine the best fit for your specific use case.

 

               1. The Open Cursor Method: A Classic Approach

The Open Cursor method in SAP ABAP is a traditional technique used for data selection. It works by opening a database cursor to fetch records in smaller, manageable sets. This approach is particularly useful when working with large datasets since it doesn't overload the system by fetching everything at once.

Syntax -

OPEN CURSOR [WITH HOLD]    cursor_name     FOR

SELECT      fields     FROM      source      

[WHERE    condition]       

[GROUP BY fields]                

[HAVING   cond]                                

[ORDER BY fields].

the WITH HOLD attribute prevents a cursor from closing when a database commit occurs in Native SQL.

the WHERE condition will help to fetch the filter data from the database

the GROUP BY will create groups based on the value of the mentioned field

HAVING will add more filtration to the data extracted

ORDER BY is used to sort the data with the fields mentioned, the default sort order is ascending, and one can use DESC to sort in descending.

Example:

 

If you use OPEN CURSOR and CALL FUNCTION 'DB_COMMIT', use the addition "WITH HOLD". The database commit won't close the cursors. NB: COMMIT WORK always closes all opened cursors.

This method ensures that only small chunks of data are fetched, which helps prevent memory overload and improves system stability when dealing with large datasets. However, it requires careful manual control of the cursor, making it less convenient compared to more modern approaches.

 

Strengths:

- Suitable for processing large datasets in small chunks.

- Prevents memory overload by limiting data fetched in each iteration.

Drawbacks:

- Requires manual cursor management.

- Lacks advanced performance optimization compared to newer techniques.

 

 

               2. Core Data Services (CDS) Views: A Modern, High-Performance Approach

CDS Views represent a modern approach to data selection in SAP, offering a more optimized and abstract way of handling data retrieval. CDS views allow you to define models at the database level using SQL-like syntax, making complex queries more manageable and improving performance. Designed to fully exploit the capabilities of the SAP HANA database, CDS views provide the ability to perform high-level operations directly on the database.

Example:

 

They can be further used to create rich CDS views ready for consumption and can primarily used with analytics tools and reporting applications like SAP Analysis for Office, Lumira, and the S/4HANA Query Browser, allowing you to expose data from your business model for analysis and visualization in various reporting formats, they are designed to be consumed by different analytical applications by providing a structured data model with specific annotations for data interpretation.

CDS views are highly efficient because they allow for powerful data manipulation at the database level, reducing the need for complex logic in the ABAP layer. They are also well-integrated with newer SAP technologies like SAP Fiori and S/4HANA.

 

Strengths:

- Optimized for performance, especially with SAP HANA.

- Declarative, SQL-like syntax makes it easier to handle complex queries.

- Provides advanced features like annotations, associations, and computed fields.

- Seamlessly integrates with modern SAP technologies.

Drawbacks:

- Requires learning and understanding of CDS concepts.

- May not be suitable for legacy systems without SAP HANA.

 

 

               3. Database Views: A Simple, Traditional Tool

Database Views in SAP provide a virtual representation of one or more database tables. While database views are not as feature-rich as CDS views, they offer a simple and effective way to combine and access related data from multiple tables. They are ideal for straightforward joins and data extraction, especially when performance is not the primary concern.

Example:

Where Database_view is the Database view created using transaction SE11.

Although database views do not provide the same level of performance optimization or flexibility as CDS views, they still serve as a useful tool in certain scenarios, particularly for simpler queries.

 

Strengths:

- Easy to define and use.

- Useful for basic data access and reporting.

Drawbacks:

- Lacks advanced capabilities like calculated fields or associations.

- Performance is lower compared to CDS views, especially for complex queries.

 

 

               4. A Comparative Overview: Choosing the Right Approach

 

The following table summarizes the key differences between the three data selection techniques discussed:

Feature

Open Cursor

CDS Views

Database Views

Performance

Medium

High

Low to Medium

Memory Usage

Low

High, but optimized for large sets

Low to Medium

Support for Complex Queries

Low

High

Low

Ease of Use

Medium

Medium to High

High

Maintainability

Medium

High

Medium

Compatibility with Modern SAP

Low

High (SAP HANA, S/4HANA, Fiori)

Low

Handling Large Datasets

High

High

Medium

 

 

               5. Advanced Data Selection Techniques and Best Practices

While the three techniques above are commonly used for data selection, several advanced strategies can further optimize performance in SAP environments:

a) Buffered Data Reads

SAP offers table buffering to speed up data retrieval for frequently accessed data. By storing table data in memory, buffering reduces the load on the database, leading to faster data access for small-to-medium-sized tables.

b) Parallel Processing

For larger datasets or more complex operations, parallel processing can distribute the workload across multiple threads. This approach can significantly reduce processing time for time-consuming operations such as joins or aggregations.

c) AMDP (ABAP Managed Database Procedures)

For scenarios requiring complex calculations or database-specific optimizations, AMDP allows you to write SQL Script (or other database-specific languages) directly in ABAP, moving the computation to the database level. This is particularly powerful for SAP HANA users, where database-side processing is much faster.

 

# Conclusion:

Each data selection technique in SAP has its advantages and ideal use cases. The Open Cursor method is a reliable choice when working with large datasets incrementally but lacks modern optimizations. CDS Views are the best choice for high performance and flexibility, especially in environments using SAP HANA or S/4HANA. Database Views offer a simpler alternative for less complex queries, though their performance and flexibility are more limited.

 

By understanding the strengths and limitations of these techniques, SAP developers can make informed decisions to optimize their applications for performance and maintainability.

Stay tuned for more insights on advanced SAP development techniques, including CDS Consumption Views in our next article!

 

"Rich CDS Consumption Views in SAP are a versatile and powerful tool for optimizing data access, especially in environments with complex data models or real-time processing needs. They are particularly beneficial for Fiori applications, real-time analytics, ABAP development, and secure data management, all while boosting performance and reducing development complexity"

 

I hope this article was helpful and added new insights to your skillset.
Thank you for reading!
Happy learning! 😊

entry.title: Re: Difficulties Accessing Excel Workbook in Build Proces...
entry.content:
Hello,

The same approach should follow:
Open Excel Instance -> Open Workbook (not Open Workbook from file) (indicate the filepath) -> "do your processing" Activate Worksheet -> Copy Cells etc tec - > Close Workbook (with save) - > Close Excel Instance

The problem you have is that, you are extracting the data without activating the Worksheet where the data is coming from. 

I hope this helps.

-Weisson

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - Business Content MM
entry.content:
You can find details about Datasphere business content here https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/6eb1eff34e4c4b1f90adfbfba1334240/a87964c26bfc1014a79e69594ccc91ad.html
but, it looks like there is nothing about MM yet.

entry.title: How can I maintain decimals in calculating Frequency in days under posting parameters in RE-FX?
entry.content: I am trying to set a frequency for a condition in a contract but I want the system to calculate the days in decimal places instead of 30 days every month and Exact numbers of Days.

entry.title: Re: Workzone Header toolbar functions not working
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

we had an open ticket with SAP on this issue. SAP has released a new version that fixes the problem. SAPUI5 version 1.131.1 has just been released.

Kind regards

Thomas Deubel

entry.title: Connecting Cloud ALM with BPA and configuring, triggering of automation in BPA upon job failure
entry.content:
Logon to BTP cockpit

Navigate to Overview section

Take note of Tenant ID and Subaccount ID

Connecting Cloud ALM to BPA :

Log on to SAP Cloud ALM tenant

Navigate to Administration -> Landscape Management

Click on the Service

Choose Add -> New Service

Enter relevant details as below in the window that pops up

The Root URL and Logon URL are the URL's from the lobby page of BPA. Please omit /lobby before using it in the Root URL and Logon URL.

Click Save.

Scroll to ENDPOINTS

Click Add

Next step is to obtain the Service key

Logon to SAP BTP Cockpit.  

  •  Navigate to the subaccount where SAP Build Process Automation is subscribed.
  • Select Instances and Subscriptions. 
  •  Select the service instance for SAP Build Process Automation.
  •  Select tab Service Keys. 
  •  Select Actions --> Download.

Create API Key

  •  Logon to SAP Build Process Automation. 
  •  Select Control Tower.
  •  Create an API key with scopes trigger_read and trigger_execute for the environments you want to use.
  •  Copy the generated API key.

While generating the API key activate

trigger_read

trigger_execute

Activate for all environments

Save the generated Key on a notepad

Return back to CALM screen and add endpoint

Once setup, Ping connection to test.

Congratulations. You have not successfully connected Cloud ALM with BPA.

As a next step, if a job fails in CALM then it needs to trigger the automation built in BPA. Let's configure this now!

Let's create a trigger in BPA.

Log into BPA

Select project that needs to be triggered upon job failure in CALM

Create -> Process

Click on Add a trigger -> API Trigger

Create API trigger

Add a process step

Choose Automation that needs to be triggered 

Choose the automation that needs to be triggered and Save it

Most important step is to release and deploy it. Else the trigger will not be identified by CALM

Click on Editable and deploy it.

Now let's set up CALM to trigger the automation in BPA upon job failure:

Login to CALM

Navigate to Operations -> Operation Automation

Register Operational Flow -> SAP Build Process Automation

End point gets automatically identified

Process Automation section, choose the Trigger that we built in BPA

Use Case : Job & Automation Monitoring

We now need to set up the Job and Automation Monitoring.

Operations -> Job & Automation Monitoring

Choose System

Choose the job that needs to be monitored

Click on Settings -> System

Click Events -> Add

Start Operational Flow -> Add -> Add Operational Flow

Select the Configured Trigger and Save it

Congratulation, We have now set up connectivity between SAP Cloud ALM and BPA and also configured triggering automation flows in BPA upon job failure.

entry.title: Re: Workzone Header toolbar functions not working
entry.content: I have tried with your guide and everything works well now. Thank you so much. 😍😍😍

entry.title: Re: How to automate SAP BTP Platform User management?
entry.content: Hello Sunil, if I did understand your comment correctly, does the new functionality solve your question? Provisioning of members in the Cloud Foundry environment (organizations and spaces) using Identity Provisioning : https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?BA=42F2E964FAAF1EDB80C512AD4FEB20ED&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=E78B0E4728D31EED9BE4ECC0931FF88B

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Agile Implementation using Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi Sri,

I do not have much experience regarding SAP SuccessFactors projects. But I know SAP Cloud ALM quite well. Therefore, let me provide a more generic answer to your question:

In SAP Cloud ALM, customers can follow the SAP Activate Methodology for SAP SuccessFactors (see the read-only version in: Roadmap Viewer - SAP for Me). This methodology consists of ~300 tasks. You can use SAP Cloud ALM to manage these tasks (read the description, access accelerators, assign team members or groups of team members, document the task status, do reporting, etc.).

Configuration Definition is part of the Explore phase. Solution Configuration belongs to the Realize phase.

 

In addition, the SAP Activate methodology provides some templates (Accelerators). However, the content of documents like requirement or user stories are highly customer- and partner specific.

Best regards, Cay

entry.title: Re: Outbound/Inbound Delivery Creation from Transportation Management
entry.content:
Hi Ranadeep,
thanks for sharing experience in that area with your blog post on this topic.

From a customer requirement it is requested to change key parameters i.e. shipping condition, INCOTERM and others from the FO generated delivery proposal, which should overrule the standard SD to LE copying control, as in LE-TRA this would also be possible. Do you have any experience on that also or suggestion on how this could be realized?

Would also appreciate feedback from anyone in the community who faced a similar customer requirement.

Best regards
Gregor

entry.title: Need V4 Fiori Element Application Reference
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are working on new requirements for developing Fiori Element Application using V4 OData.

It has to be break-out type with extension on Actions.

Can someone suggest which Example we can take as a reference with the Git link.

Regards,

Rewati Raman

entry.title: Basic Credit Management (BD6): Exlcude a payment term (prepayments) from credit limit calculation
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

Using Basic Credit Management (BD6) for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. I would like to know if it is possible to exclude a specific payment term from the credit limit calculation.

Specifically, we want to exclude a specific payment term for payments made in advance (prepayments).
Our goal is that a sales order created with this payment term will never be cheked against a customer's credit limit.

Thank you for your help.

Best regards,
Simon

entry.title: MRN2 devaluation indicator update
entry.content:
Hello, 

Is it possible to make a mass change to update the devaluation indicator in MRN2, the user updated in change made the wrong values and wants to revert back to the previous settings. 

many thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - How to use Filter component in examples
entry.content: Please use just $iflowname

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hello Suzanne

I'm from India. I have cleared one of my SAP certifications in Mar'24 and the other one in Aug'24. Do I need to take SAP learning Hub subscription and take the assessment exam to stay certified? Still there is almost 3 months time though. As currently I am unemployed and such situation doesn't allow an individual to subscribe. What is the solution under such conditions?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Tax number IN0 is required for Customer : tax number...
entry.content:
Hello @thangarajthomas 

Please refer to the SAP Note below if the issue persists

CVIS_UI_GENERIC001

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Signavio Dictionary integration with ServiceNow
entry.content:
Hi,
additionally, you can also connect ServiceNow via our SAP Signavio Process Manager API:

  • SAP Signavio Process Manager API Guide
  • Postman Collection
  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub

You can also use our existing integration with SAP Risk and Assurance Management via SAP Integration Suite as blueprint as they also interact with the Dictionary.

Can you share more details about your use case? Do you want to push data to the Dictionary or retrieve data from Signavio? 

Feel free to send me a message via this platform with your e-mail address to discuss further. 

Best regards
Manuel

entry.title: Servicecontract & Prcicing with cummluatve condition
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

we are using S4-SERVICE modul for Serviceorder, Inhouse Repair, Spare Parts and want to implement Service Contracts. In Servicecontract we have the requirements that prices are maintained on child items and the header item will be invocied. Theefore we need to cummulate the prcie to header item level. In SD modul this is a very common proess and condition type KUMU is used with formula 36 (invclude FV64A036) is used.

Unfortunatelly this is not working for Servicecontract. The Header Item condition KUMU is not updated.

 

Any ideas?

Do we need to develope own formula?

 

 

Regards

Andreas

entry.title: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Value is required, null given for key: ug!
EWM Warehouse Management Monitor – Enhancements with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2
entry.content:
The Warehouse Management Monitor in EWM offers a wide range of flexibility for customizing existing monitor node content or creating own monitor content. The how-to guide “How To Add Application Content to the Warehouse Management Monitor” (see note 3004810) guides developers in how to use these options by customizing the standard monitor, adding their own application content to the standard monitor or to completely creating their own monitors / nodes etc.

However, so far the options to do simple enhancements of existing standard nodes, e.g. adding one or two additional fields like customer-defined EEW (Easy Enhancement Workbench) fields, always required* creating customer-specific nodes: It is necessary to create own copies of the existing standard nodes and the underlying source code, and to extend those with the required additional fields. Drawback: You have created Z-coding for the nodes and enhancements or corrections for the EWM standard nodes won’t automatically apply to your copies of the nodes.

*Exception: Nodes that already support “Dynamic Selections” for additional fields, e.g. the Documents nodes for Warehouse Order / Warehouse Task or the Stock and Bin -> Storage Bin node.

With OP2023 FPS2, the new feature “Monitor Screen BAdIs” for the warehouse monitor is introduced, which provides a simplified approach for extending standard nodes without requiring a copy of the standard monitor node anymore. By implementing the new monitor screen BAdI “BAdI: Custom Selection Fields in Standard Monitor Nodes” (/SCWM/EX_MON_CUSTOM_SELECT), it is possible extend the selection screens of selected standard monitor nodes with customer-defined fields, and to either integrate them into the standard selection logic, or to implement a customer-specific selection logic for the new fields that can be combined with the standard selection logic. This new feature is described in the latest version of the Monitor How-To guide, version 3.0.

Example

Example Use Case: You have extended Inbound Deliveries with a customer-specific EEW-Field “Priority” and Handling Units with a customer-specific EEW-Field “Preferred Putaway Destination”. You want to use both fields in the warehouse management monitor to be able to select e.g. Inbound Deliveries of priority 1 and 2, and to select Handling Units relevant for such deliveries that are intended to go to specific putaway destinations in your warehouse.

Prerequisite: You have already created domains and data elements for the EEW fields and extended the structures with the respective fields. You have also defined logical field names for the delivery specific EEW fields. You have implemented the necessary application logic to write and read the new fields during Inbound Delivery and Handling Unit processing.

You can use the new Monitor Screen BAdIs to enhance the Inbound Delivery selection screen with the new selection field for “Priority”, and the selection screen for the Handling Unit subnode with both the additional parent selection field “Priority” and the HU specific selection field for the “Preferred putaway destination”. The extended screens could look like this:

Figure 1 - Standard Inbound Delivery selection screen with customer specific Screen Enhancement

Figure 2 - Standard Inbound Delivery -> HU selection screen with customer specific Screen Enhancement

Steps to extend a Monitor Node with a Screen BAdI

In the latest version of the Monitor How-To Guide, you can find a detailed example of how to enhance an existing standard monitor node with customer specific fields. In addition, you can always check the example implementations for the new BAdI for guidance on how to implement each BAdI method per node. For the example use case described above, you can e.g. check  /SCWM/CL_EI_MON_CUST_SEL_INB.

Roughly summarized, the following needs to be done to enhance the standard nodes for the example Use Case described above:

  1. Creation of a BAdI implementation and implementing class, specific for your warehouse number and the technical names of the to-be-extended nodes as filters: /SCWM/WHRITEM_MON + /SCWM/WHRHEAD_INB_HU_MON.
    • Recommendation: Implement one BAdI for hierarchical monitor nodes, e.g. Inbound or Outbound Delivery Order and their respective sub nodes. This ensures that the defined selection screens and selection fields can be reused and are handled consistently for all nodes.
  2. Creation of a Z-report to define the customer specific selection screen for your EEW-fields, and to implement the screen and variant data handling. The selection screen will be included in the standard monitor node in a new subscreen area, which is located below the standard selection screens. Check the details in the Monitor How-To Guide and the comments in the example implementations for detailed information how to implement the Z-report including the sub screen.

Please note:

  • For simplicity, we will only describe the variant with EEW fields in this document, but you can use the following fields for extending the selection screens:
    1.      Standard fields for the objects that are not included in the standard selection screen
    2.      EEW-fields added to the EEW include of the relevant objects
  • If you use such fields, they can be considered in the standard selection of the monitor node. Other fields won’t be considered, as there are validity checks in place that will filter out fields that can’t be supported by the standard selection logic.
  • For the example described in this document, you can therefore use fields from /SCWM/S_WIP_Q_WHR_INBOUND (incl. EEW fields) for the Inbound Delivery selection screen, and /SCWM/S_HUHDR_MON (incl. EEW fields) for the HU selection screen.
  1. Implementation of the BAdI methods according to your use case.
    Please note: For most nodes, it is sufficient to implement the BAdI and the customer specific report to provide the mapping for the additional EEW-fields to the standard node. The standard selection logic can directly consider the additional selection values. This does not apply for HU-specific selections, though – for HU-specific nodes, an additional customer-specific pre-selection must be implemented in the BAdI. You can find details in the Monitor How-To Guide and also in the node specific example implementations.  
    • The pre-selection logic should return HU keys that are relevant based on the customer selection values. It is recommended to carefully design this pre-selection logic, as it might have an impact on the performance of the overall node execution. Please note that if the pre-selection does not return any results, the standard selection will not continue, either.
    • The maximum number of returned HU keys is 32.000, if this is exceeded the monitor selection will not continue
    • The pre-selection logic will only be called if customer specific selection fields are used. If only standard fields are used, the standard selection will be executed and the pre-selection logic will be skipped.
  2. Specifying the screen enhancements in the BADI implementation, to connect standard screen and custom subscreen area from the Z-report.

You can find the screens that should be enhanced with to custom subscreen area in the BAdI Implementation section “Screen Enhancement”

Figure 3 - Example Screen Enhancements for Inbound Delivery node examples

Conclusion

With the new Monitor Screen BAdI feature, simple selection screen enhancements of existing standard monitor nodes are now possible without the need to copy existing nodes to customer nodes. This reduces the overhead when implementing corrections or upgrading, as the standard nodes will get any corrections and enhancements, and no customer nodes need to be adapted in addition.

Monitor nodes extended by customer selection fields still behave like any other standard monitor node, e.g. you can use standard functionalities like setting up automatic refresh, creating selection variants for the node incl. the customer selection fields, scheduling background executions of the variant selections, etc., if the Screen BAdI is correctly implemented.

In which cases does it make sense to use the Screen BAdI?

  • Simple enhancements of existing nodes, where standard selections can include the fields, e.g. Inbound Delivery Items with EEW fields
  • Simple enhancements of existing nodes, where HU pre-selection can be implemented in a simple way to pre-select values based on customer fields (e.g. selecting HUs with additional standard selection fields, e.g. open WTs, or HU EEW fields).

In which cases does it not make sense?

  • Enhancements of nodes with fields that are not supported by the original node selection, e.g. because the requirement is to combine selection information of multiple objects, or the selection fields is not even a standard field (e.g. enhancing the HU document bin selection with a field for number of completed warehouse tasks)
  • Enhancements of nodes with a complex set of selection criteria, or special requirements for the selection that does not match the logic of the standard monitor node.

In theses cases, the creation of an own monitor node with a customized selection logic is recommended. The Monitor How-To Guide provides detailed examples for the creation of customer specific nodes as well.

FIND OUT MORE!

To see more details on the above mentioned topics (such as examples of configuration screens), as well as see what other enhancements we delivered with this release, I highly encourage you to explore our blog SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Extended Warehouse Management Private Edition 2023 FPS02– What’s New .

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information about SAP Extended Warehouse Management, please follow us on social media, our YouTube channel or our community pages:

SAP EWM Community

SAP YouTube Channel

EWM LinkedIn Community

SAP Extended Warehouse Management Roadmap Explorer

Release information and restrictions of embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

For a complete list of Q&A from the EWM community, please access this link:
https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000705

In case you do not find your specific question there, feel free to post your question via the following form:
https://answers.sap.com/questions/ask.html?primaryTagId=01200615320800000705

entry.title: How to convert SAP module pool programs and screens into SAP BTP?
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am new to this area and have developed an application in the ECC on-premises system. I would like to deploy it onto SAP BTP. From my research, I have found that SAP BTP does not support Classic Workflow, BDC, Classical FM, classical programming, ALV Grid Controls, module pool programs/screens, Web Dynpro components, SAP Script and Smart Forms, or any custom development objects.

The application I developed in the ECC system includes some of the objects mentioned above. I am currently analyzing it using the custom code migration app from the SAP BTP ABAP Environment.

My question is, since SAP BTP does not support the objects listed above, how can we convert them for use in SAP BTP?  or how can we make that application cloud-ready? Is there a specific tool or extension available from SAP to facilitate this conversion? 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: IPS transformation assignGroup and multiple groups assignment
entry.content:
I see... In the documentation they put both constants in one "call"...

did you try enabling the *.support.patch.operation?

Cause maybe if he's doing a "PUT" it is always "overwriting" everything, Patch could avoid that...

entry.title: Re: Employee as contact person
entry.content: in our system is not visible. But we have an answer. If you see list of contacts and got to MORE there you should see "Create from Business Partner" and you can select there Employee. In that way new contact is created which is also employee (have both roles)

entry.title: Re: BTP FinOps: Keeping Track of your Credits
entry.content:
Hello @Willem_Pardaens ,

Thanks for providing the out of box open source app. That's really helpful in some aspects as compared to standard cockpit costs & usage. We have implemented it in our BTP environment.
1. About Managed tags, on what basis do we tag the % based on LOB? Does it really look into the actual usage of XYZ LOB and service usage consumption units for those users? OR as a app user we define the % with static values?
2. Maximum times when I load my app, the stats on overview page does not appear, it gives "404 Not Found Try to refresh later or contact technical support"

Best Regards,

Abhishek Ghodke

entry.title: Issue with Employee History Salary Field for Hourly Pay Component in Variable Pay
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

I’m facing an issue related to Variable Pay, and I’m wondering if anyone else has encountered the same problem.

The "Salary" field in Employee History is mapped to the sum of a Pay Component Group. This Pay Component Group has several associated pay components in EC: one with an hourly frequency. When the employee has a monthly pay component, the value is annualized correctly and pulled into the Employee History. However, when the employee has the hourly pay component, the salary shows as 0 in the Employee History.

In EC, everything looks fine, and the Pay Component Group is annualizing correctly for both the monthly and hourly pay components. However, the Employee History remains at 0.

Has anyone faced this issue before, or does anyone have any suggestions for resolving it?

entry.title: Re: IPS transformation assignGroup and multiple groups assignment
entry.content: Yes

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Agile Implementation using Cloud ALM
entry.content: Thanks @cayrademann for the reply. I have went through the roadmap viewer and now its quite clear about the way it works. It was mentioned in the documents, that a starter system is provided for the fit to standard workshops, is it available for SuccessFactors as well. I understand that you do not have much experience in SuccessFactors but if you are aware, please let me know.

entry.title: How to check which Auth Checks a program in SAP does
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am looking for a way on how to find all AUTHORITY-CHECKs for a given transaction, report or Fiori App without manually looking through the code.

I have encountered countless situations where I added a new tile for a transaction or Fiori app and then user complained about not having access to certain information because of missing authorization settings in their role. PFCG is already a powerfull tool but doesn't always catch all auth checks since it (afaik) relies on presets for the auth objects in the SAP system.

Recently we added a 3rd party software from a company to our collection and it was my duty to create all the new tiles. Since I am getting tired of "maintaining PFCG, transport role, user complains, check SU53, maintain PFCG again, transport again, rinse and repeat" I would like to ask: Is there a already implemented transaction by SAP that I can give a transaction or report name and it spits out all the auth checks so I have a list  of "all auth checks for that report"?.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content:
@Michał_B 

How you distinguish between class member table sales_orders and local table sales_orders ?
mt_ and lt_ gives you immediate sign - local or class member variable, so i know that i'm modifying a local variable and not the class member...

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Agile Implementation using Cloud ALM
entry.content: Hi Sri, sorry, I am not aware if there is an SAP SuccessFactors starter system or not. Best regards, Cay

entry.title: Re: Budget Transfer Timing for STO Transactions
entry.content: Thank you for your suggestion! I’m currently looking into it and will get back to you soon.

entry.title: Re: Success Factors Employee Central - set 'Show More' as...
entry.content:
Hi

Before turning on the people profile this was easily accessible, but have not found a workaround after the update on People Profile and the also Configure People Profile.

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content:
@rosenpetrovIMO if you have trouble distinguishing between a local variable and a class member your problem isn't solved by HN but by choosing better names in general.

Silly example but think about this:
Within a methode, the lifetime of a variable is very short, someone could say it's temporary, so why not use sales_orders for the class member variable and temp_customer_sales for the local one? Yea i know temp_ is basically a malformed HN but I hope you get the idea 😉 .

entry.title: Can Employee Central be configured to show all info expanded by default?
entry.content: After updating to the new People Profile, sections that were fully visible before now require clicking 'Show More.' Is there a way to configure Employee Central to display all information fully expanded by default? I’ve checked Configure People Profile but couldn’t find an option. Any advice?

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content:
Easy way out: Use "me->" if you need to make it clear.

me->sales_orders = sales_orders.

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content:
@rosenpetrov 

How you distinguish between class member table sales_orders and local table sales_orders ? mt_ and lt_ gives you immediate sign - local or class member variable, so i know that i'm modifying a local variable and not the class member...

Personally I use me-> or classname=> for accessing class members.

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content:
@StephanZiemerTHIS TBH!

C# uses this, VB.NET uses Me, ABAP has the me keyword

I haven't done OOP ABAP, but I have read some OOP code. Totally forgot about that 😅
